meta.dataset: On The Door — pilot council sources registry
meta.regime_note: England & Wales: Cumulative Impact Policies/Assessments under Licensing Act 2003 (CIAs renew every 3 years, SoLPs every 5). Scotland: overprovision assessments under Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005.

status_key.tightening: New/expanded CIA, SCZ, or live consultation likely to restrict
status_key.stable: Policy in force, no active change
status_key.loosening: CIA/overprovision removed or presumption of refusal dropped

councils.name: Westminster City Council
councils.policy: SoLP consultation open for 2026 revision; draft keeps West End CIZ (slightly reduced NE of Covent Garden) and adds large new Special Consideration Zones

live_consultation.subject: Statement of Licensing Policy 2026 + Cumulative Impact Assessment

councils.notes: NTIA has publicly branded the consultation potentially unlawful (April 2026) — live dispute, strong story for launch issue.

councils.name: Hackney Council
councils.policy: SoLP effective 1 Aug 2023 (max 5 yrs, review due by Aug 2028). CIA for Shoreditch & Dalston consulted; adoption not yet reflected on council policy page

live_consultation.subject: CIA consultation closed; VERIFY adoption status

councils.notes: Consultation majority supported CIA but flagged economic/cultural concerns. NEEDS VERIFICATION: whether CIA formally published.

councils.name: Camden Council
councils.policy: SoLP 2025–2030 adopted; cumulative impact areas (Camden Town, Seven Dials) REMOVED — committee found insufficient evidence for a CIA
councils.notes: Resident groups (Camden Town TRAs) unhappy about CIA removal. New later-hours framework, women's safety policies added.

councils.name: Tower Hamlets Council
councils.policy: CIA 2024–2027 in force (20 Nov 2024 – 20 Nov 2027): Brick Lane CIA retained and expanded SE; Bethnal Green CIA removed
councils.notes: 3-year CIA cycle — next review consultation likely mid-2027.

councils.name: Islington Council
councils.policy: SoLP 2023–2027 in force with six cumulative impact areas incl. Archway; review due 2027
cia_zones: Six CIAs incl. Archway (see SoLP for full list)
councils.notes: 2027 SoLP renewal is a watch item — consultation likely late 2026/early 2027.

councils.name: Southwark Council
councils.policy: SoLP 2026–2031 adopted (early 2026) with dedicated CIA chapter: Borough & Bankside CIA, Camberwell CIA; proposal to extend Late Night Levy to late-night refreshment premises (00:00–06:00)
councils.notes: 114 LNR premises in CIAs; levy extension worth ~£127k. Corsica Studios closure (Mar 2026) sits in this borough.

councils.name: Lambeth Council
councils.policy: SoLP 2024–2029 in force (30 Jan 2024 – 29 Jan 2029); cumulative impact policy covers part of Clapham High Street with rebuttable presumption of refusal

councils.name: Manchester City Council
councils.policy: SoLP 2024–2029 in force; NO cumulative impact assessment — flexible merits-based approach with 'late night economy' phase defined (00:00–04:00+)
councils.notes: Deliberately open stance — useful contrast case for the tracker.

councils.name: Bristol City Council
councils.policy: SoLP 2025–2030 effective 1 Aug 2025; CIA in effect from 8 Mar 2024 (city centre retained after 2023 near-removal; Bedminster/Southville CIA consulted separately)
councils.notes: Live licence review: El Rincón, North Street (police-requested, Jul 2026).

councils.name: Birmingham City Council
councils.policy: New licensing policy implemented 1 May 2026; Broad Street and city centre remain cumulative impact areas with stricter scrutiny
councils.notes: NEEDS VERIFICATION: exact CIA boundaries in the 1 May 2026 policy — council CMIS links are unstable, pull the policy PDF directly.

councils.name: Leeds City Council
councils.policy: CIA 2026–2028 approved by Licensing Committee Jan 2026; Beeston CIA added Jan 2025; expanded again 2025 to include Hyde Park end of Headingley Lane
councils.notes: Full 232-page CIA only available by email request (entertainment.licensing@leeds.gov.uk) — good example of how scattered/inaccessible this data is.

councils.name: Newcastle City Council
councils.policy: SoLP 2023–2028 in force (from 1 Nov 2023); review introduced 5 new cumulative impact policy areas for off-licences and late-night refreshment

councils.name: Liverpool City Council
councils.policy: All cumulative impact policy areas REMOVED at 2021 policy review; SoLP approved 20 Jan 2021 — 5-year renewal is now due/overdue (2026)
councils.notes: SoLP renewal consultation should surface in 2026 — high-priority watch item for the weekly agent.

councils.name: Brighton & Hove City Council
councils.policy: SoLP 2026 adopted 18 Dec 2025, effective 5 Jan 2026: CIZ scrapped and replaced with City Safety Area (same boundary, no blanket presumption of refusal, matrix-based scrutiny); Special Stress Area retained; new 'good operator' policy
councils.notes: First council to rebrand CIZ as a safety-first framework — a structural story, not just a boundary change.

councils.name: City of Glasgow Licensing Board
councils.policy: Licensing Policy Statement 2023–2028 in force with proactive overprovision assessment; overprovision areas mapped on council site

councils.name: City of Edinburgh Licensing Board
councils.policy: Overprovision policy SCRAPPED by 5–3 board vote, 23 Jun 2025 (supplementary policy statement adopted after statutory consultation); former overprovision areas: Old Town, Princes Street, Tollcross, Leith Street
councils.notes: Police Scotland, NHS Lothian and Alcohol Focus Scotland gave evidence against removal — contested decision, follow-up story potential.
